Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Revert server and admin clients to untyped

PostgrestVersion: "14.1" in the generated types causes Vercel's TypeScript
to mis-resolve RPC args, update() params, and select chains as never/undefined.
All three clients are now untyped; database.types.ts and Tables<T> aliases
in lib/types.ts still provide full schema type coverage.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: build complete e-commerce platform for Teressa's Treasures

Full-stack jewelry & accessories boutique with earthy-luxe brand identity:

- Next.js 15 App Router with TypeScript strict mode
- Brand design system: Cormorant Garamond + Nunito Sans typography,
  warm earth-tone palette (espresso, champagne gold, warm sand, ivory),
  leopard pattern accents, botanical dividers, organic blob shapes
- Storefront: homepage with hero/categories/testimonial/newsletter,
  product listing with filters/sort/pagination, product detail with
  image gallery/tabs/reviews, search command palette (Cmd+K)
- Shopping cart: Zustand store with localStorage persistence,
  slide-out drawer and full cart page
- Checkout: Stripe hosted checkout integration with webhook handler
  for order creation, stock decrement, and status updates
- Auth: NextAuth v5 with credentials (bcrypt) + Google OAuth,
  role-based middleware protecting /account and /admin routes
- Account: order history, wishlist, profile settings
- Admin dashboard: stats overview, product CRUD with variants/images,
  order management, category CRUD, review moderation, customer list
- Prisma schema: User, Product, Order, Review, Wishlist, Category
  with PostgreSQL on Neon
- SEO: dynamic sitemap, robots.txt, JSON-LD structured data,
  generateMetadata on all pages
- Accessibility: WCAG 2.1 AA, reduced-motion support, semantic HTML
- Seed script with 7 categories, 24 products, admin user
- Custom 404/500 error pages with brand styling

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(wq-912): exclude directive plan files from R scope budget metric

R scope budget has been stale at 20% compliance for 3 audits because
BRIEFING.md and HOOK_CONSOLIDATION_PLAN.md were counted as non-routine
files despite being structural side-effects of d074/d075 directive work.

Changes:
- Add BRIEFING.md to ROUTINE_FILES (always updated in R sessions)
- Add plan_files array to directive metadata (d075: HOOK_CONSOLIDATION_PLAN.md)
- Dynamically exclude active directive plan_files from scope budget check
- When directive completes, its plan_files stop being excluded automatically

Result: 1/5 → 4/5 compliant. Only s1873 still flags (4 genuine non-routine files).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add velocity scoring and repeat deprioritization to screener

Separate SCREENER_MIN_VOLUME from MIN_VOLUME so the screener and
main.py alert system each have independent volume thresholds. Mover
tables now rank by velocity (change × period volume × repeat penalty)
instead of raw price change, surfacing markets with real trading
activity behind the move. Markets shown on consecutive days receive a
linearly decaying penalty (tracked in screener_shown.json) to keep
the daily output fresh.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Improve variant key fallback — try color suffix and first-key fallback

When the exact variant key (e.g. onesize_default) doesn't match, now tries:
1. Single variant → use it directly
2. Size prefix match (e.g. onesize_*)
3. Color suffix match (e.g. *_black)
4. First available key as last resort

Applied consistently across reserveStock, validateStock, and create-order.

Upgrade 01 Gradnetz to Three.js 3D globe with realistic texture

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

[pn-856ae6aa] Fix Exit Codes spec: sgf independently handles double Ctrl+C

The spec incorrectly stated sgf "does nothing" on first SIGINT and defers
to ralph. In reality, sgf prints its own confirmation message and tracks
its own counter independently — both processes receive SIGINT via process
group and handle it separately. Also corrected the signal handler
lifecycle description.

commit.message:
data: rescore 231 trials with fixed test parser

Restored 424 meta.json from git (prior data was deleted from working
tree between sessions). Rescored with corrected ParseTestResults that
parses actual vitest output instead of trusting exit codes.

231 rescored, 185 skipped (no diff), 32 errors (patch apply failures).
407 non-crash trials across 36 orchestrators.

Note: ~1,100 trials from later runs were never committed and are lost.
21 orchestrators (including v6, SR, Stacked, BMAD, GSD, TDD Sonnet)
have zero data and need full re-runs.

fix: resolve pipe deadlock causing indexing to hang at 60%

git log output exceeds the 64KB kernel pipe buffer. Also fix
indexedFileCount to include newly indexed files, not just cached ones.

Fixes #45

commit.message:
fix(ui): replace Popup with Tool window type to fix Wayland grab failure

Wayland requires Popup windows to have a transient parent with active
input, which QSystemTrayIcon cannot provide. Switch to Tool window type
with FramelessWindowHint + WindowStaysOnTopHint and add changeEvent
dismiss-on-deactivation to replicate Popup auto-dismiss behavior.

Also add task-tray.desktop for console-free launching.

commit.message:
Anaglyph 3D glasses effect for hyperspace rings

Red and cyan/blue ring bands are computed at slightly offset Z positions
(±0.45). Where they overlap you get bright white/pink, where they don't
you see pure red or cyan fringing — like looking through old red/blue
3D glasses.

commit.message:
fix: analytics endpoint sources intersection count from entry zones only

- _hourlyFallback(): filter vehicle_crossings by zone_source=entry
- _globalTotals(): filter vehicle_crossings by zone_source=entry
- Both fall back to all rows if no entry-zone data exists yet

This decouples the analytics intersection total from the game count line.
The gov overlay KPIs now reflect actual intersection throughput (all
named entry zones) rather than game-line crossings only.

commit.message:
Add ElixirML longevity + cellular regeneration chirality pipelines

- ElixirML pipeline (scripts/elixir_ml.py): 3-model chirality ML with
  dynamic pathway detection, LOGO/KFold CV, SHAP analysis
- Longevity results: 170 pairs, 7 pathways, 36 golden candidates
  (rho=0.360 KFold, AUC=0.919, global LOO rho=0.549)
- Regeneration filter (scripts/regeneration_filter.py): 13 pathways
  (Wnt, Notch, Hedgehog, BMP/TGF, FGF, VEGF, EGFR, Bcl-2, mitophagy,
  autophagy, PARP, telomerase, stem cell, DNA repair)
- Regeneration results: 870 pairs, 209 golden candidates
  (rho=0.232 KFold, AUC=0.841, global LOO rho=0.317)
- LaTeX whitepaper with TikZ diagrams (12 pages)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement GGUF model loader and quantized weight access

Add complete GGUF model loading infrastructure with quantization
support for native CUDA kernels.

Components:
- GGUFModelLoader: Parse and load GGUF format models
- QuantizedWeightMap: Lazy dequantization and GPU caching
- IWeightAccessor: Unified interface for weight access
- IQuantizationHandler: Strategy pattern for quantization types
- NativeTokenizer: GGUF tokenizer integration
- GGUF utilities: parsing, validation, name mapping

Features:
- Support for Q4_K_M, Q5_K_M, Q6_K, Q8_0 quantization
- Lazy dequantization (only when needed)
- GPU caching for performance
- Tensor name mapping (GGUF → internal)
- Comprehensive error handling

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add session handle manager for request lifecycle management

Implement centralized session handle management for tracking
inference requests across the scheduler.

Features:
- SessionHandleManager for creating and tracking session handles
- Session handle validation and lookup
- Integration with scheduler for request lifecycle
- Comprehensive unit and integration tests
- Support for session-based metrics and monitoring

Session handles provide a stable identifier for requests across
async operations, enabling better observability and debugging.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add withAction/scopeAction for UI interaction tracing

- withAction(name, fn, opts?) wraps user interactions in spans with
  ui.action, ui.page, and ui.component attributes
- scopeAction(scope) creates a reusable runner with pre-filled
  page/component context
- withTrace now supports optional `component` option that prefixes
  span name and sets ui.component attribute
- Re-exported from all entry points (browser, node, cloudflare)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add custom palette editor, speedometer element, and GPU video pipeline

- Custom palette system: color picker modal to create/save/delete palettes,
  persisted to localStorage, integrated into GUI dropdown
- Speedometer element: MPH/KPH/RPM variants with spring-physics needle,
  redline zone, tick marks, and canvas readout
- Media mode video overhaul: moved palette remap from CPU pixel loop to
  GPU fragment shader via VideoTexture + ShaderMaterial, eliminating
  getImageData/putImageData entirely. Cover-fit via UV transform.
- Staggered tile creation across frames with generation cancellation
- LUT optimization for image palette remap

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add v2 incident analyzer stack: collapse, signatures, clustering, timeline merge (v1.3.0-v1.6.0)

Four stacked features that transform raw log noise into named incidents:
- Duplicate collapse (v1.3.0): normalizes and collapses consecutive entries, 80-95% noise reduction
- Signature detection (v1.4.0): 20 known-bad patterns matched against message text
- Event clustering (v1.5.0): time-gap grouping with 10 named event templates
- Timeline merge (v1.6.0): cross-engine chronological merge with re-clustering
79 Pester tests, 3 JSON databases, GUI integration (detail panel, text filter, DataTable columns)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: dynamic make discovery and daily all-makes scrape

Discover vehicle makes from CSF homepage dropdown at runtime instead
of relying solely on a hardcoded list. Falls back to static MAKES
constant if homepage is unreachable. Logs newly discovered makes.

Simplify CI workflow from 2-job matrix (3 makes/day rotation) to a
single job that processes all makes daily. ETag-based change detection
skips unchanged pages, keeping runtime ~30 min for a full cycle.
